episode0.title: THE DEAD ENGINEER — DIRECTOR'S CUT V4

event3.text: Before we go near it, I need you to understand something.

event5.text: Curiosity is how this station got most of its scars.

event7.text: Scientists call it discovery. Engineers call it troubleshooting. Management calls it initiative.

event9.text: The rest of us called it Tuesday.

event12.text: I'm leaving the relay room now. Personnel cache is two decks down, medical archive is one deck past that.

event14.text: If the map is telling the truth, we can get answers without touching 7.14.

event1.text: You are going straight for the thing I asked you not to touch.

event3.text: I respect the consistency. I hate the survival instinct.

event6.text: 7.14 was a backup emergency carrier. Quiet frequency, low traffic, ugly signal profile.

event8.text: The kind of thing you build because redundancy looks responsible in a report.

event11.text: Then it answered before anyone transmitted.

event13.text: A backup channel should not talk first.

event15.text: If it answers us again, remember this was not a radio. It was a door handle.

event3.text: I hate that the unknown voice gave useful advice. That feels unfair.

event6.text: For the record, if a haunted signal gives better procedural guidance than I do, I am filing a formal complaint.

event10.text: Personnel cache door is ahead. Half open. That is never charming.

event3.text: Door is jammed halfway open. Enough for me to squeeze through, not enough for dignity.

event5.text: There's a manual crank on the wall and a maintenance panel under the frame.

event7.text: Crank is faster. Panel is safer. I already know which one you'll pick and I'm ready to judge you silently.

event6.text: Also, I pinched my glove seal. Not a rupture. Just the station reminding me it hates initiative.

event8.text: Air hissed for half a second. My heart did something deeply unprofessional.

event4.text: Found a bypass strip. Someone used this recently.

event6.text: Not station-recent. Human-recent. Scrape marks, glove residue, a tiny smear of sealant.

event8.text: Whoever opened this did not want the door log to notice.

event10.text: I'm inside. Cache terminals are live.

event0.title: PERSONNEL FILE: ETHAN HALE
event0.content:
DR. ETHAN HALE
ROLE: CHIEF ENGINEER
ASSIGNMENT: ORBITAL RESEARCH STATION ECHO
STATUS: DECEASED
TIME OF DEATH: 21:20
LOCATION: DOOR 12 ACCESS CORRIDOR
event0.caption: Recovered personnel record from corrupted station cache.
event0.message: [PERSONNEL FILE: ETHAN HALE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event10.text: I just needed the universe to make a different choice the second time.

event15.text: I've been called a lot of things.

event17.text: Chief Engineer. Workaholic. Condescending, once, by a man who used the word 'synergy' unironically.

event21.text: Do I sound dead to you?

event23.text: That was not rhetorical. I am genuinely asking because my confidence in basic categories is collapsing.

event3.text: I am trying very hard not to think about what that means while continuing to breathe.

event5.text: Which is an extremely inconvenient thing to do while looking at your own death certificate.

event7.text: There should be a manual for this. 'So You've Been Officially Dead for Months.'

event10.text: I'm moving to medical archive. If this file is wrong, medical should contradict it.

event15.text: Then I don't know what I am.

event3.text: That is probably the least scientific comfort available, and right now I am choosing to treasure it.

event5.text: I can hear the relay hum. I can feel the deck vibrating under my boots.

event7.text: I am annoyed by you, which is historically a living-person activity.

event9.text: So let's keep that theory alive for as long as we can.

event11.text: I'm heading to medical archive. We verify this before I start haunting anything.

event1.text: Medical archive is past the observation corridor.

event3.text: Two routes. Main corridor is direct but exposed. Lab access is slower and probably full of things with warning labels.

event5.text: I cannot believe I miss normal warning labels.

event4.text: Something moved in the ceiling while I was halfway across.

event6.text: Not a rat. Before you ask. Too heavy.

event8.text: I did not look up. I am learning.

event10.text: Medical archive door is ahead. My pulse is making editorial comments.

event1.text: Lab route. Slower. Less ceiling horror, theoretically.

event4.text: There are mugs still magnetized to the wall rack.

event6.text: Lin's mug is here. Tiny cartoon moon on it. She hated that mug and used it every day out of spite.

event11.text: Bad coffee. Cold pizza. Someone complaining the gravity sim made their knees feel old.

event3.text: I would like to state in advance that I hate every word in that sentence.

event4.title: AUTOPSY FRAGMENT: HALE
event4.content:
SUBJECT: ETHAN HALE
CAUSE OF DEATH: DECOMPRESSION TRAUMA
LOCATION: DOOR 12 ACCESS CORRIDOR
RECOVERY STATUS: BODY NOT RECOVERED
NOTE: VITALS TERMINATED AT 21:20
ARCHIVE INTEGRITY: 68%
event4.caption: Recovered emergency medical fragment.
event4.message: [AUTOPSY FRAGMENT: HALE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event12.text: If they found a body, at least the nightmare has a shape.

event14.text: But not recovered means the station either lost me...

event16.text: ...or it found something it refused to call a body.

event18.text: Listen to me. I sound like a man arguing with a filing cabinet.

event20.text: This is exactly why I hated medical logs. They make tragedy sound organized.

event5.text: I know pieces. I just don't know if they're mine.

event8.text: I remember the door. The handle. The warning light above it.

event10.text: I remember the seal making this soft little click. Ridiculous thing to remember, isn't it?

event12.text: Not the death. Not the person. The click.

event15.text: There was shouting. Someone said my name like I had already done something unforgivable.

event17.text: Then a pressure drop. Or the memory of one.

event1.title: VOICE SIGNATURE REPORT
event1.content:
ACTIVE SPEAKER: ETHAN HALE
VOICEPRINT MATCH: 84%
SIGNAL DELAY: NONLINEAR
CARRIER TRACE: 7.14 MHz harmonic residue detected
event1.caption: Recovered voiceprint comparison from live relay traffic.
event1.message: [VOICE SIGNATURE REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event5.text: That is just high enough to ruin my day and low enough to avoid answering anything.

event9.text: Wonderful. Even my voice is doing suspicious things now.

event0.title: TIMELINE CONFLICT
event0.content:
21:14 — Door 12 opened.
21:17 — Security override accepted.
21:20 — Ethan Hale declared deceased.
21:22 — Live communication signature still matches Ethan Hale.

Conflict: active communication signature exists after recorded time of death.
Secondary conflict: communication signature contains 7.14 MHz harmonic residue.
event0.caption: Automated timeline conflict report.

event4.text: A neat little contradiction with timestamps and labels.

event6.text: The system says I died at 21:20. The relay says I kept talking after.

event8.text: And somewhere between those two statements, I am supposed to exist.

event11.text: We don't need another puzzle. We need a decision.

event13.text: Either the record is wrong, or I am not the only thing using my name.

choice0.text: TREAT THE DEATH RECORD AS CORRUPTED.

choice1.text: TREAT THE LIVE SIGNAL AS COMPROMISED.

event1.text: Record corrupted. I like that theory.

event3.text: It is emotionally convenient, which makes it suspicious, but I like it.

event5.text: If the file is wrong, then I am alive and the station is broken.

event12.text: I hate that it waits until I'm almost calm.

event6.text: It hurts, but fair and painless rarely share a room.

event8.text: If my signal is compromised, then every word I send you could be a door opening from the wrong side.

event10.text: I need you to remember something.

event12.text: If I start sounding too calm, too certain, too helpful... question me.

event6.text: I wish I hadn't, but I did.

event8.text: A dead engineer with no body. A living signal with my voice. A channel leaving fingerprints.

event10.text: I would like to formally resign from being a mystery.

event3.text: Movement is useful. Panic is just cardio with worse branding.

event6.text: I'm at the carrier control point.

event8.text: The channel is there. Not open. Waiting.

event10.text: I do not like systems that wait.

event7.text: You listened to it. I understand why. I hate that I understand why.

event8.title: SIGNAL NOISE
event8.completeText: [HALE REACHED EMERGENCY CARRIER CONTROL / SIGNAL DEGRADED]

event10.text: I'm at the carrier control point. Signal is dirty now. We brought some of it with us.

event3.text: There was another voice on the emergency channel.

event5.text: Not the unknown one. Not exactly.

event7.text: It sounded close. Like hearing someone through a wall and realizing they know your name.

event13.text: Operator... I don't think we're opening a frequency.

event15.text: I think we're about to meet somebody.

event4.text: No— I said stabilize it first.

event6.text: The channel is open now. We did not lose anyone. But we lost control of how it opens.

event8.text: There is a difference between unlocking a door and having something pull it from the other side.

event5.text: I never thought I would say that. I have mocked slow systems professionally.

event7.text: But right now slow feels like a handrail.
